title: Constructor and destructor declarations should not be redundant
description: Constructor and destructor declarations should not be redundantAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEfindingclumsyWhy is this an issue?Since the compiler will automatically invoke the base type’s no-argument constructor, there’s no need to specify its invocation explicitly. Also, when only a singlepublicparameterless constructor is defined in a class, then that constructor can be removed because the compiler would generate it automatically. Similarly, emptystaticconstructors and empty destructors are also wasted keystrokes.Noncompliant code exampleclass X { public X() { } // Noncompliant static X() { } // Noncompliant ~X() { } // Noncompliant ..
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects empty public parameterless constructors, empty static constructors, empty destructors, and explicit base() calls
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when constructor or destructor declarations are empty (only braces, no code) OR when base() is explicitly called without arguments
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes private constructors, constructors with parameters, constructors with any code inside, and base calls with arguments
logic.reasoning: Redundant constructors and destructors add unnecessary code clutter. The compiler automatically generates default parameterless constructors, so explicit empty ones are redundant. Empty static constructors and destructors serve no purpose. Explicitly calling base() without arguments is redundant as it's done automatically. Private constructors are excluded as they're used to prevent instantiation (singleton pattern). Constructors with code are valid initialization.
